mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate lifts discount rates and tightens financial conditions, which lowers S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when the increase is part of a sustained tightening path.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 pp over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 20d return after a 0.25 pp increase is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate indicates improving labor income and consumption capacity, which supports equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if a 0.2 pp decline in UNRATE is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and housing starts signals broad demand acceleration, improving revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either proxy alone, or if conditional 20d returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is accelerating while the policy rate remains elevated, real rates and recession risk rise together, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates for 2 consecutive monthly prints while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-tight-policy condition does not lead to negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger real output and better corporate operating leverage, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are not followed by above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional average return is negative over any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening cyclical demand and deteriorating credit-sensitive activity, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if an >8% housing-starts drop is not followed by negative 60d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal loses directional accuracy relative to unconditional chance.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth slows while unemployment rises, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to compress earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth is below 0.1% month-over-month and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 pp in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-slowdown and unemployment-rise condition does not precede negative 20d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-reopening period, faster GDP growth combined with lower unemployment signals a durable demand recovery that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with improving GDPC1 and falling UNRATE do not generate above-median 60d SPX returns, or if the joint signal is not stronger than either component alone.
